g.text: SDA_RX), can be either I2C or UART.

g.desc: SDA_RX), can be either I2C or UART.

g.text: * D+ and D- is bypassed to the host,

g.desc: * D+ and D- is bypassed to the host,

g.text: but no MUX controls it. Host must

g.desc: but no MUX controls it. Host must

g.text: communicate with the board to know

g.desc: communicate with the board to know

g.text: when it's safe to establish USB

g.desc: when it's safe to establish USB

g.text: * The pinout is intended to be unified.

g.desc: * The pinout is intended to be unified.

g.text: However, this board doesn't have a

g.desc: However, this board doesn't have a

g.text: up by BQ25620's PMIC/OTG when on

g.desc: up by BQ25620's PMIC/OTG when on

g.text: which can be up to 20V. Maximum

g.desc: which can be up to 20V. Maximum

g.text: can be configured at firmware level.

g.desc: can be configured at firmware level.

g.text: * WARNING: Both VOTG and VBUS can be

g.desc: * WARNING: Both VOTG and VBUS can be

g.text: presented on VEXT! All other supply

g.desc: presented on VEXT! All other supply

g.text: * For external extensibility (e.g. add OLED and buttons, etc.). Adds a "HAT" to the Board.

g.desc: * For external extensibility (e.g. add OLED and buttons, etc.). Adds a "HAT" to the Board.

g.text: * Board has two modes of operation: Standalone and Managed. In Managed Mode, only PHER bus

g.desc: * Board has two modes of operation: Standalone and Managed. In Managed Mode, only PHER bus

g.text: should be used by the HAT/shield. In Standalone mode, both PHER and USER buses can

g.desc: should be used by the HAT/shield. In Standalone mode, both PHER and USER buses can

g.text: be used safely. This is because in PHER, the board is Master; but in USER, board is Slave,

g.desc: be used safely. This is because in PHER, the board is Master; but in USER, board is Slave,

g.text: unless it's in Standalone mode. See the SoC (soc-control) sub-sheet for more details.

g.desc: unless it's in Standalone mode. See the SoC (soc-control) sub-sheet for more details.
